name: Mississippi Mud Bars
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Line a 9 by 13 inch baking pan with aluminum foil, extending the edge of the foil 1 inch over the sides of the pan. Grease and flour the foil, then set aside.
3. In a medium saucepan, melt 1 cup butter. Stir in 1 1/2 cups sugar and 1/2 cup cocoa powder until well combined.
4. Remove the pan from heat and beat in eggs one at a time until blended. Stir in flour and chopped pecans until well mixed.
5. Pour the batter into the prepared pan and bake for 25 minutes or until a cake tester inserted into the center comes out clean. Allow to cool on a wire rack.
6. For the frosting, combine confectioners' sugar and cocoa powder in a medium bowl. Beat in melted butter and vanilla extract until smooth.
7. Gradually beat in milk (1 to 2 tablespoons) until the frosting is fluffy and spreadable.
8. Using the ends of foil, lift the brownie layer from the pan. Spread the frosting evenly over the top.
9. Sprinkle with an additional 1/2 cup chopped pecans.
10. Cut into 2 by 1 inch bars and store in an airtight container.
